彌種継命 |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= 津島ノ宮 |例祭= |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''草創神社'''（くさわけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]]上田 (上田市)|…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 草創神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=上田市}} |所在地= [[徳島県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]字弥伍平267 |祭神= [[須佐之男命]]・
彌種継命 |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= 津島ノ宮 |例祭= |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''草創神社'''（くさわけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』[[角川書店]]、[[1990年]]。ISBN 4040012003。[[尾張国]]津島（現：[[愛知県]][[対馬市]]）の[[津島神社]]の分霊を勧請したのが始まりで、当初は津島ノ宮と称していた。 この地の開拓の祖で[[真田氏]]の金井飛騨守の崇敬が篤かったが、[[村上義清]]によって社領が没収され、後に[[武田晴信]]によって七貫文の社地が寄進された。 [[寛永]]年間（[[1848年]]-[[1855年]]）に津島神社境内摂社である彌五郎社を勧請し、「彌伍宮」と呼ばれ、[[明治維新]]後に郷社に列した。[[1901年]]（[[明治]]34年）[[4月11日]]草創神社と改称した[[1979年]],[[平凡社]]『長野県の地名』。 == 祭神 == * [[須佐之男命]] * 彌種継命 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[上田駅]]」より車で約10分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[上田菅平インターチェンジ]]」より車で約5分。 == 脚注 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさわけしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

revision.comment: 徳島県→長野県,対馬市→津島市,所在地に出典,角川に{{要ページ番号}}(本社に関する単独項目なし)
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024-09}} {{神社 |名称= 草創神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=上田市}} |所在地= [[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]字弥伍平267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=神社紹介 上小支部 |publisher=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} |祭神= [[須佐之男命]]・
彌種継命 |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= 津島ノ宮 |例祭= |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''草創神社'''（くさわけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 20 長野県』[[角川書店]]、[[1990年]]。ISBN 4040012003 {{要ページ番号|date=2024-09}}。[[尾張国]]津島（現：[[愛知県]][[津島市]]）の[[津島神社]]の分霊を勧請したのが始まりで、当初は津島ノ宮と称していた。 この地の開拓の祖で[[真田氏]]の金井飛騨守の崇敬が篤かったが、[[村上義清]]によって社領が没収され、後に[[武田晴信]]によって七貫文の社地が寄進された。 [[寛永]]年間（[[1848年]]-[[1855年]]）に津島神社境内摂社である彌五郎社を勧請し、「彌伍宮」と呼ばれ、[[明治維新]]後に郷社に列した。[[1901年]]（[[明治]]34年）[[4月11日]]草創神社と改称した[[1979年]],[[平凡社]]『長野県の地名』250頁。 == 祭神 == * [[須佐之男命]] * 彌種継命 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[上田駅]]」より車で約10分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[上田菅平インターチェンジ]]」より車で約5分。 == 脚注 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさわけしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

revision.comment: 『角川日本地名大辞典 20 長野県』(頁数記載なし)を確認したが記載が確認できず初版投稿者からの説明もないため([[利用者‐会話:2404:7A84:140:1F00:81E:1E6A:97EA:8DDE]])

'''Sōzō Shrine''' (草創神社, also read Kusawake Shrine)
* {{ill|Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|草創神社 (上田市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, Ueda. * {{ill|Sōzō Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|草創神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, Kataue Town. * {{ill|Sōzō Shrine (Komatsushima City)|ja|草創神社 (小松島市)}} - Located in Tokushima Prefecture, {{ill|Komatsushima City|ja|小松島市}}, Maehara Town.
== Related Items == * {{ill|Sōzōmori Shrine|ja|草創森神社}} - Located in Tokushima Prefecture, Tokushima City, {{ill|Hata Town (Tokushima City)|ja|八多町}}.

'''Sōzō Shrine''' (草創神社, also read Kusawake Shrine)
* {{ill|Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|草創神社 (上田市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, Ueda. * {{ill|Sōzō Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|草創神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, Kataue Town. * {{ill|Sōzō Shrine (Komatsushima City)|ja|草創神社 (小松島市)}} - Located in Tokushima Prefecture, {{ill|Komatsushima City|ja|小松島市}}, Maehara Town.
== Related Items == * {{ill|Sōzōmori Shrine|ja|草創森神社}} - Located in Tokushima Prefecture, Tokushima City, {{ill|Hata Town (Tokushima City)|ja|八多町}}.
{{DEFAULTSORT:Sōzō Shrine}} {{Disambiguation}} [[Category:kk]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]][[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] <!--note do not remove this category during translation--> [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | Name = Kusawake Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | Location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} Former Village Shrine | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Names = Tsushima-no-Miya | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place Names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[Category:kk]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]][[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate names = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]]

{{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate names = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)-qq]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)-qq]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)-qq]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]]

The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate names = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda {{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate names = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * {{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate names = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:11]]

{{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate names = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}}. == References == == External Links == * During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]]

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:草創神社 (上田市) up to rev 102428496
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024-09}} {{神社 |名称= 草創神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=上田市}} |所在地= [[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]字弥伍平267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=神社紹介 上小支部 |publisher=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} |祭神= [[須佐之男命]]
彌種継命 |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= 津島ノ宮 |例祭= |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''草創神社'''（くさわけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。[[尾張国]]津島（現：[[愛知県]][[津島市]]）の[[津島神社]]の分霊を勧請したのが始まりで、当初は津島ノ宮と称していた。 この地の開拓の祖で[[真田氏]]の金井飛騨守の崇敬が篤かったが、[[村上義清]]によって社領が没収され、後に[[武田晴信]]によって七貫文の社地が寄進された。 [[寛永]]年間（[[1848年]]-[[1855年]]）に津島神社境内摂社である彌五郎社を勧請し、「彌伍宮」と呼ばれ、[[明治維新]]後に郷社に列した。[[1901年]]（[[明治]]34年）[[4月11日]]草創神社と改称した[[1979年]],[[平凡社]]『長野県の地名』250頁。 == 祭神 == * [[須佐之男命]] * 彌種継命 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[上田駅]]」より車で約10分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[上田菅平インターチェンジ]]」より車で約5分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf 神科歴史探訪]（60頁参照） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさわけしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024-09}} {{神社 |名称= 草創神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=上田市}} |所在地= [[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]字弥伍平267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=神社紹介 上小支部 |publisher=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} |祭神= [[須佐之男命]]
彌種継命 |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= 津島ノ宮 |例祭= |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''草創神社'''（くさわけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[上田市]][[上田 (上田市)|上田]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。[[尾張国]]津島（現：[[愛知県]][[津島市]]）の[[津島神社]]の分霊を勧請したのが始まりで、当初は津島ノ宮と称していた。 この地の開拓の祖で[[真田氏]]の金井飛騨守の崇敬が篤かったが、[[村上義清]]によって社領が没収され、後に[[武田晴信]]によって七貫文の社地が寄進された。 [[寛永]]年間（[[1848年]]-[[1855年]]）に津島神社境内摂社である彌五郎社を勧請し、「彌伍宮」と呼ばれ、[[明治維新]]後に郷社に列した。[[1901年]]（[[明治]]34年）[[4月11日]]草創神社と改称した[[1979年]],[[平凡社]]『長野県の地名』250頁。 == 祭神 == * [[須佐之男命]] * 彌種継命 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[上田駅]]」より車で約10分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[上田菅平インターチェンジ]]」より車で約5分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf 神科歴史探訪]（60頁参照） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くさわけしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧郷社]] [[category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda (Ueda City)|qq=}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern typeing System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ueda City|ja|上田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)|qq=}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima City|ja|津島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima City|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|ja|真田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanada clan|qq=}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|ja|村上義清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Yoshikiyo|qq=}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei Era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei Era|qq=}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station|ja|上田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|ja|四国旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikoku Railway Company|qq=}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Joshinetsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joshinetsu Expressway|qq=}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2024-09}} {{Shinto Shrine | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda (Ueda City)|qq=}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)]] in Tsushima, [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]]. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=}} (Shingen). During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]]

Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)]] in Tsushima, [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]]. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=}} (Shingen). During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[Category:History of Ueda City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ueda City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Sōzō Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:上田市の建築物]] [[Category:上田市の歴史]] [[Category:上田市の画像提供依頼]]

Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)]] in Tsushima, [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]]. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄}} (Shingen). During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:上田市の画像提供依頼]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Sōzō Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/草創神社]] [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[sn:Sōzō Shrine (Ueda City)]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Image requests in Ueda City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kusawake-jinja (Ueda, Nagano)]][[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q134734429]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=2024-09}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Kusawake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Ueda City}} | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], {{ill|Ueda (Ueda City)|ja|上田 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda (Ueda City)|1=Ueda, Uedashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124317819}} Aza Yagohira 267{{Cite web |url=https://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/jousyou.htm |title=Shrine Introduction, Jōshō Branch |publisher=Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2024-09-09 }} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)]] in Tsushima, [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]]. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (Shingen). During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|1=Ueda-Sugadaira interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868176}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Image requests in Ueda City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q134734429}}

The shrine originated when a branch deity of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)]] in Tsushima, [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]]. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (Shingen). During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|1=Ueda-Sugadaira interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868176}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134734429}} [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Image requests in Ueda City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)]] in Tsushima, [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Tsushima, Aichi|Tsushima City]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the [[:en:Sanada clan|Sanada clan]]. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by [[:en:Murakami Yoshikiyo|Murakami Yoshikiyo]] and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (Shingen). During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei Era]] (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Ueda Station (Nagano)|Ueda Station]] on the [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|Shikoku Railway Company]]. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|1=Ueda-Sugadaira interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868176}} on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Joshinetsu Expressway]]. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134734429}} [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Image requests in Ueda City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto | type = {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Unknown | Main Hall = | alternate_name = Tsushima-no-Miya | festival = | Rituals = | Label Position = | Map = }} '''Kusawake Shrine''' (草創神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, Japan. == History == The exact year of its founding is unknown. The shrine originated when a branch deity of {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine (Aichi)}} in Tsushima, {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Tsushima, Aichi|en|Tsushima, Aichi|WD=Q816736|lt=Tsushima City}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}) was enshrined here. It was initially called "Tsushima-no-Miya." The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|en|Murakami Yoshikiyo|WD=Q1048622|lt=Murakami Yoshikiyo}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|1=Ueda-Sugadaira interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868176}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Joshinetsu Expressway}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134734429}} [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Image requests in Ueda City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The shrine was highly revered by Kanai Hida-no-Kami, the pioneer settler of the area and a retainer of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|WD=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}. However, the shrine's lands were confiscated by {{ill|Murakami Yoshikiyo|en|Murakami Yoshikiyo|WD=Q1048622|lt=Murakami Yoshikiyo|1=Murakami Yoshikiyo}} and later restored with a donation of seven kanbun worth of land by {{ill|Takeda Harunobu|ja|武田晴信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Harunobu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武田信玄|1=Takeda Shingen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276404}} (Shingen). During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei Era|1=Kan'ei}} (1848–1855), the Yagorō Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Tsushima Shrine, was enshrined within its precincts, and the shrine came to be known as "Yagomiyai." After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was designated as a village shrine. On April 11, 1901 (34th year of the Meiji era), it was renamed Kusawake Shrine.{{Cite book|year=1979|publisher=Heibonsha|title=Nagano Prefecture Place names|page=250}} == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * Yatanetsugi-no-Mikoto == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Ueda Station (Nagano)|en|Ueda Station (Nagano)|WD=Q1071375|lt=Ueda Station|1=Ueda Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=Shikoku Railway Company|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. * Approximately 5 minutes by car from the {{ill|Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|ja|上田菅平インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda-Sugadaira Interchange|1=Ueda-Sugadaira interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868176}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Joshinetsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}}. == References == == External Links == * [https://kamishina-net.com/rekishitanbo2.pdf Kamishina History Exploration] (see page 60) {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|草創神社 (上田市)|version=102428496|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:草創神社 (上田市)]] [[ja:草創神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134734429}} [[Category:Buildings in Ueda]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ueda]] [[Category:Image requests in Ueda City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
